In congruence $13(3x-2)\equiv 26 \pmod 8$
$$13(3x-2)\equiv 26 \implies 3x-2\equiv 2 \implies x\equiv  \frac{4}{3}$$
This is not possible, so there is no positive integer solution less than or equal to 20 to the congruence.
However, the problem asks for the sum of all positive integer solutions, and there are no solutions, so simply $ \boxed{0}$.
